name: New Relic MCP Server (Official)
what_it_does: Official New Relic MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with New Relic's observability platform — querying metrics and traces via NRQL, checking alerts, reading dashboards, and investigating application and infrastructure health.
use_cases: Querying application performance metrics via NRQL from monitoring agents
Checking alert policies and incident state for on-call agents
Analyzing error rates and response times during incident response
Reading infrastructure metrics from New Relic agents
Building automated runbooks using New Relic data
not_for: Teams using Datadog, Dynatrace, or other APM platforms
Log-only observability (New Relic is full-stack APM)
Agents unfamiliar with NRQL query language
best_when: An agent needs to investigate application performance, check alerts, or query observability data from a New Relic-instrumented system.
avoid_when: You're using Datadog, Dynatrace, or another APM platform — use those integrations.

auth.notes: User API keys for agent access. License keys for data ingest only. No fine-grained scopes on API keys — account-level access.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: 100GB data/month, 1 user, 8 days data retention
pricing.notes: Data ingestion pricing. Free tier useful for small services. New Relic is competitive on price vs Datadog.

performance.rate_limits: NerdGraph API: 25 concurrent requests; NRQL rate limits apply

known_agent_gotchas: NRQL is New Relic-specific SQL dialect — agents must know NRQL syntax
Account ID required for all queries — configure correctly
NerdGraph (GraphQL) vs REST API have different capabilities
Data retention limits mean historical queries beyond retention window fail
User API keys have no scope granularity — full account access
Metric data vs event data vs log data use different NRQL patterns
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. API keys lack scopes — account-level access. FedRAMP, SOC 2, ISO 27001, GDPR, HIPAA. Enterprise APM security standard.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. API key lacks scopes. FedRAMP, SOC 2, ISO 27001, GDPR, HIPAA. Enterprise observability platform.
